clinical_notes.summary: 45yo male presenting for annual wellness examination with no acute complaints. Reports mild exercise intolerance with >2 flights of stairs attributed to deconditioning. Denies chest pain, palpitations, syncope, dyspnea at rest, orthopnea, or edema. PMH: generalized anxiety disorder, well-controlled on sertraline 50mg daily. Non-smoker. Vitals: HR 74 bpm, BP 122/78 mmHg, SpO2 99%, BMI 26.1. Physical examination unremarkable. Heart sounds normal, lungs clear bilaterally.

chest_xray.summary: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality. Lung fields clear bilaterally, no infiltrates, consolidation, or effusions. Heart size normal with cardiothoracic ratio 0.48. Mediastinum unremarkable. No bony abnormalities.

ecg.summary: Normal sinus rhythm at 74 bpm. Normal axis. PR interval 156ms (normal). QRS duration 86ms (normal). No ST-segment changes. No T-wave abnormalities. QTc 402ms (normal). No conduction abnormalities. Entirely unremarkable tracing.

echo.summary: Normal left ventricular size and function, LVEF 60%. No wall motion abnormalities. Normal diastolic function. No significant valvular disease. Right ventricular size and function normal. No pericardial effusion. Left atrial size normal at 3.6cm.

heart_sounds.summary: S1 and S2 normal intensity and splitting. Regular rate and rhythm at 74 bpm. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No S3 or S4 detected. Heart sounds entirely within normal limits.

lab_results.summary: CBC within normal limits. WBC 6.8 x10³/µL. Hemoglobin 14.6 g/dL. BMP normal: sodium 139 mEq/L, creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, eGFR >90 mL/min. Fasting glucose 92 mg/dL (normal). Total cholesterol 188 mg/dL, LDL 112 mg/dL, HDL 52 mg/dL. Triglycerides 124 mg/dL. TSH 1.8 mIU/L (normal). No abnormal findings.

medication.summary: Active medications: Sertraline 50mg daily (generalized anxiety disorder). No cardiac medications. Daily multivitamin. Allergy: sulfa drugs (rash). No drug-drug interactions. Medication review unremarkable. No new medications indicated at this visit.
